question: The constant friction between the two countries might ______ a full-scale war.
explanation:
• **Bring about**: To cause something to happen. 🌀
• **Bring up**: To raise a child or mention a topic. 👶
• **Bring in**: To introduce a law or earn money. 💵
• **Bring off**: To succeed in something difficult. 🏆

question: I have always ______ my elder brother because of his integrity and hard work.
explanation:
• **Looked up to**: To admire or respect someone. 🎖️
• **Looked down on**: To consider someone inferior. 🤨
• **Looked after**: To take care of. 🏥
• **Looked into**: To investigate. 🔎

question: The proposed deal ______ because the two companies could not agree on the price.
explanation:
• **Fall through**: To fail to happen (plans/deals). 📉
• **Fall out**: To quarrel. 😤
• **Fall off**: To decrease in quality or quantity. 📉
• **Fall back**: To retreat. 🚶

question: The company decided to ______ fifty workers due to the sudden economic recession.
explanation:
• **Lay off**: To dismiss workers. 💼
• **Lay down**: To sacrifice or establish rules. ⚖️
• **Lay out**: To spend money or arrange. 💰
• **Lay in**: To store for future use. 📦

question: He is highly skilled, but he is simply not ______ the stress of a surgical career.
explanation:
• **Cut out for**: To be suited for something. 👔
• **Cut down on**: To reduce consumption. 📉
• **Cut in on**: To interrupt. 🗣️
• **Cut off from**: To isolate. 🔌

question: It is difficult to ______ what he is saying because of the loud background music.
explanation:
• **Make out**: To understand or hear with difficulty. 👂
• **Make up**: To invent a story or reconcile. 📖
• **Make for**: To head towards a place. 🏃
• **Make over**: To transform or legally transfer. 🔄

question: The government is planning to ______ old laws that are no longer relevant to modern society.
explanation:
• **Do away with**: To abolish or get rid of. 🗑️
• **Do up**: To renovate or fasten. 🛠️
• **Do with**: To need or use. 🤝
• **Do without**: To manage without something. 🚫

question: The doctors are hopeful that the patient will ______ after the major surgery.
explanation:
• **Pull through**: To recover from illness or danger. 🏥
• **Pull up**: To stop a vehicle. 🚗
• **Pull off**: To succeed in a difficult task. ✅
• **Pull down**: To demolish a building. 🏗️

question: She tried her best to ______ for the time she lost while she was sick.
explanation:
• **Make up for**: To compensate for. ⚖️
• **Make out**: To discern or understand. 👂
• **Make up**: To create or reconcile. ✨
• **Make off**: To leave in a hurry. 🏃

question: The situation requires immediate action; this problem ______ a firm decision from the board.
explanation:
• **Calls for**: To require or demand. 📢
• **Calls on**: To visit or ask someone to speak. 🚪
• **Calls off**: To cancel. ❌
• **Calls up**: To recall or telephone. ☎️

question: You must ______ your work if you want to pass the exam with high marks.
explanation:
• **Keep on**: To continue. ➡️
• **Keep off**: To stay away from. 🚫
• **Keep away**: To maintain distance. 🚷
• **Keep out**: To prevent from entering. 🚪

question: The scientist has been ______ research on climate change for over a decade.
explanation:
• **Carry out**: To perform or execute. ⚙️
• **Carry on**: To continue. ➡️
• **Carry off**: To win or handle a situation. 🏆
• **Carry through**: To complete successfully despite difficulty. ✅

question: He finally had to ______ and admit that he was wrong after seeing the evidence.
explanation:
• **Give in**: To yield or surrender. 🙇
• **Give up**: To stop trying. 🏳️
• **Give away**: To reveal a secret. 🎁
• **Give out**: To stop working or distribute. 💔

question: Before submitting the report, make sure to ______ it for any grammatical errors.
explanation:
• **Go over**: To review or examine. 🔎
• **Go by**: To follow rules. ⏳
• **Go for**: To choose or attack. 🎯
• **Go off**: To explode or ring. ⏰

question: How do you manage to ______ on such a small monthly salary in this expensive city?
explanation:
• **Get by**: To survive with little money. 💵
• **Get over**: To recover. ❤️‍🩹
• **Get along**: To be friendly. 👫
• **Get away**: To escape or go on holiday. ✈️

question: It took him nearly six months to ______ the loss of his beloved pet.
explanation:
• **Get over**: To recover emotionally. ❤️‍🩹
• **Get by**: To manage/survive. 💵
• **Get away**: To escape. 🏃
• **Get through**: To finish or reach someone. 📞

question: If we don't start walking faster, we will never ______ the rest of the group.
explanation:
• **Catch up with**: To reach the same level/place. 🏃‍♂️
• **Catch on**: To understand or become popular. 🧠
• **Catch at**: To try to grab. ✋
• **Catch out**: To discover someone's mistake. 🔎

question: Rare coins are difficult to ______, but they are worth a fortune to collectors.
explanation:
• **Come by**: To obtain something difficult. 🛍️
• **Come across**: To find by chance. 🔍
• **Come about**: To happen. 🌀
• **Come along**: To progress or accompany. 🚶

question: The detective is trying to ______ how the thief managed to bypass the high-tech security.
explanation:
• **Figure out**: To understand or solve. 🧠
• **Figure on**: To expect or plan. 📅
• **Figure in**: To include in a calculation. ➕
• **Figure up**: To calculate a total. 📊

question: We are deeply saddened to inform you that the veteran actor ______ last night.
explanation:
• **Passed away**: To die. 🕊️
• **Passed out**: To lose consciousness. 😵
• **Passed by**: To go past without stopping. 🚶
• **Passed on**: To transmit or die (less formal). ➡️

question: The dark clouds suggest that the monsoon has finally ______ in the coastal regions.
explanation:
• **Set in**: To begin and likely continue (unpleasant weather). 🌧️
• **Set off**: To start a journey or explode. ✈️
• **Set up**: To establish or arrange. 🏗️
• **Set out**: To start with a specific aim. 🎯

question: Could you ______ me in the meeting? I might need your help with the data presentation.
explanation:
• **Stand by**: To support or be ready. 🤝
• **Stand for**: To represent or tolerate. 🏛️
• **Stand out**: To be very noticeable. 🌟
• **Stand up**: To rise or fail to meet someone. 🕴️

question: You ______ your grandmother; you have the same sharp features and kind eyes.
explanation:
• **Take after**: To resemble a relative. 👨‍👦
• **Take off**: To depart or succeed. 🛫
• **Take in**: To understand or deceive. 🧠
• **Take over**: To take control. 👑

question: The new manager is ready to ______ the challenge of turning the company around.
explanation:
• **Take on**: To accept responsibility/challenge. ⚔️
• **Take up**: To start a hobby or occupy space. 🎨
• **Take in**: To observe or understand. 👁️
• **Take out**: To remove or escort someone. 🚪

question: It ______ that the suspicious package was just a harmless box of old books.
explanation:
• **Turned out**: To result in or be discovered as. 🏭
• **Turned down**: To reject. 👎
• **Turned up**: To arrive. 🔊
• **Turned off**: To stop a device. 🔌

question: In a luxury hotel, there are always staff members ready to ______ your every need.
explanation:
• **Wait on**: To serve someone. 🍽️
• **Wait for**: To await someone/something. ⏳
• **Wait up**: To stay awake waiting. 🌙
• **Wait out**: To wait until something ends. 🕒

question: I need to ______ more often if I want to stay healthy and fit.
explanation:
• **Work out**: To exercise or solve. 🏋️
• **Work up**: To develop a feeling (like appetite). 🍛
• **Work on**: To try to improve or repair. 🛠️
• **Work off**: To get rid of (debt/stress) through effort. 💨

question: Make sure you ______ your files regularly so you don't lose your work if the computer crashes.
explanation:
• **Back up**: To make a copy of data. 💾
• **Back out**: To withdraw from a deal. 🚶
• **Back down**: To admit defeat. 🙇
• **Back off**: To move away from a fight. ✋

question: The speaker struggled to ______ his complex ideas to the young audience.
explanation:
• **Put across**: To communicate clearly. 🗣️
• **Put out**: To extinguish or annoy. 🧯
• **Put off**: To postpone. 🗓️
• **Put up**: To build or stay somewhere. 🏗️

question: We need to ______ a solution to this problem before the deadline tomorrow.
explanation:
• **Work out**: To find a solution. 💡
• **Work on**: To spend time improving. 🛠️
• **Work for**: To be employed by. 🏢
• **Work in**: To find time for something. 🕒
